prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
In the third quester of this anno domini of ethereal vapors, the SaaS barons proclaimed their coffers to have swollen by a score-and-ten percentum, as if the very gold of Midas had been conjured from the aether by some unholy incantation upon their "cloud."

Lo, the horde of subscribers did multiply like the plagues of Egypt, swelling to nigh upon a hundred thousand souls bound in fealty to the glowing talismans of software, though I wot not what sorcery doth bind them so.

Yet a grievous woe befell the realm, wherein a fiendish "churn rate" devoured fifteen percent of the faithful, as if invisible imps spirited away the vassals to rival fiefdoms peddling cheaper elixirs of service.

Thus spake the high priests of the board, foretelling a bountiful harvest in the morrow's quarter, provided the capricious gods of algorithm withhold not their thunderous outages upon the sacred servers.
